name: Parsnip Potato Puree
recipeIngredient: 1 Pound parsnips, peeled and quartered crosswise
1 Large russet potato, (about 6 ounces), peeled and quartered
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
2 Ounces unsalted butter, room temperature, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the parsnips and potato in a medium pot and cover with cold salted water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
2. Reduce heat and simmer until very tender and almost falling apart, about 20 minutes. Drain.
3. Transfer vegetables to a large holed sieve set over the same pot.
4. Mash the vegetables through the sieve using a mallet or large spoon in an up-and-down motion. Set aside at room temperature.
5. To serve, warm the puree over medium-high heat with a wooden spatula until heated through. Add butter and stir until smooth. This can be prepared 1 hour ahead and kept warm in a larger pan of gently simmering water (bain marie), stirring occasionally.
6. Mix in parsley. Serve immediately on vegetable dishes or individual plates. Yields 2 to 4 servings.
